text: Commute
Congestion
A detour
A roundabout
Rush hour
A one-way street
To hail a cab
Gridlock
A crosswalk
Public transit
A shortcut
A pedestrian
To pull over
The driver who knew a shortcut saved us time.
We took a route that avoided the one-way streets.
He pulled over at the corner where the pedestrians were waiting.
There is always heavy congestion on the bridge.
We left early to avoid the rush hour.
Always wait at the crosswalk until the light turns green.
The driver who knew a shortcut saved us twenty minutes.
We got stuck in total gridlock at rush hour.
The public transit, which is fast and cheap, is the best choice.
He pulled over at the crosswalk to let me out.
The public transit here is fast and cheap.
This is the corner where you can hail a cab.

First, she got lost in a city where the congestion was terrible. Then she hailed a cab, which soon got stuck in total gridlock. The driver, who was very patient, found a shortcut around the one-way streets. He pulled over at a crosswalk and pointed her toward the metro. Finally, she took the public transit, which was faster and much cheaper.
